rationale: Career spans Civil/Mechanical engineering and Sales at non-product companies (TCS, Acme Corp) with no ML or IR production work; only surface-level AI interest via online courses on RAG/vector DBs noted in summary.

Entire career is in non-technical roles (Graphic Designer, Operations Manager, Accountant) at manufacturing/IT-services firms with no engineering or ML work whatsoever despite holding two postgraduate ML/AI degrees.

Career entirely at IT-services firms (Tech Mahindra, Wipro) and fictional company Dunder Mifflin doing test automation and DevOps, with no production ML/IR work to substantiate the advanced Recommendation Systems and LLMs skill claims.

Only 2.4 years experience, current role at fictional company Stark Industries doing test automation, with Qdrant listed as an intermediate skill (29 months) but no evidence of production embedding/retrieval deployment.

Strong Learning-to-Rank and Feature Engineering skills (both advanced, 38 months) and real product-company experience at Ola building Kafka/Spark streaming pipelines, but only 2.6 years total experience with no demonstrated production embedding or vector-search deployment.

Sales Executive career at manufacturing (Globex) and IT-services (Infosys) companies focused on operations and business analysis, with no ML engineering work; AI interest limited to online courses mentioned in summary.

Career entirely in marketing and operations roles at fictional companies (Initech, Wayne Enterprises) and Wipro IT services with no engineering or ML production work, despite some tech skill listings.

7.9-year career spent entirely as Accountant and Content Writer at Wipro, Wayne Enterprises, and Acme Corp with zero engineering or ML work; tech skills listed (Node.js, FastAPI, Azure) are unsupported by any relevant role descriptions.

Entire 3.4-year career at Cognizant (IT services) doing Android mobile development with no production IR/ranking work; advanced Reinforcement Learning skill (51 months) is implausible given the role descriptions and does not align with the search/ranking requirements.

Sales Executive and Graphic Designer career entirely at fictional companies (Initech, Stark Industries) with no ML, engineering, or IR work; skill set is entry-level web/infra with no relevance to the role.
